name: Umbria (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the stylised regional logo, evoking the green hills and forests of Umbria, the green heart of Italy
the stylised regional logo, evoking the red brick and tile of Umbria's medieval hill towns

description: A white field charged at the centre with the regional logo of Umbria: three stylised interlocking arched figures rendered in green and red.
history: Codified by Regional Law n. 1 of 16 April 2005 (Article 4 of the Regional Statute). The flag uses the modern regional logo, designed by graphic studio Studio FM, which became the visual identity of Umbria from the late 1990s.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the regional logo, a stylised composition of three interlocking arched figures (the Cerchi) in red and green.

features.role: the three Cerchi (interlocking arched figures of the regional logo)
features.symbolism: The three interlocking figures of the regional logo evoke the Umbrian hills and the historic three constituents of the medieval region; the green-and-red palette references its nickname as the green heart of Italy and its terracotta-coloured towns.
